summary: Add Elevation Profile Data to Trail Response
description:
Add elevation gain, elevation loss, and elevation profile array to the GET /trails/{id} response.

Currently the trail response only includes basic metadata (name, difficulty, distance). Guests and the mobile app need elevation data to properly display trail profiles and help guests plan their hikes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- GET /trails/{id} includes elevationGainMeters (number)
- GET /trails/{id} includes elevationLossMeters (number)
- GET /trails/{id} includes elevationProfile (array of {distanceKm, elevationMeters} points)
- Elevation data is sourced from the trail-geo-data service
- Response time does not degrade beyond current p95 (200ms)

summary: Adventure Category Classification for Check-In UI Patterns
description:
Implement adventure category classification to drive different check-in UI patterns based on adventure type.

The check-in service currently uses a single UI flow for all adventure types. Different categories (guided tours, self-service trails, equipment-required activities) need distinct check-in patterns:
- Guided tours: group assembly, guide assignment, safety briefing confirmation
- Self-service trails: wristband scan, waiver confirmation, trail map distribution
- Equipment activities: equipment checkout, fitting verification, safety gear check

The adventure catalog service should expose a 'category' field that the check-in UI consumes to determine which flow to render.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Adventure catalog API returns category enum: GUIDED_TOUR, SELF_SERVICE, EQUIPMENT_REQUIRED
- Check-in service reads category and selects appropriate UI pattern
- Fallback to SELF_SERVICE if category is missing or unknown
- Category classification rules are configurable without code deployment

summary: Guide Schedule Overwrite Bug in Scheduling Orchestrator
description:
The scheduling orchestrator is overwriting guide enrichment data when processing concurrent schedule updates.

Reported Issue:
When two schedule updates arrive within a short window for the same guide, the second update overwrites the enrichment data (certifications, language skills, specializations) attached by the first update. This causes guides to appear unqualified for adventures they are certified for.

Impact:
- Guides are being removed from adventure assignments they are qualified for
- Manual re-assignment is required, causing operational delays
- Approximately 15-20 incorrect removals per day during peak scheduling periods

Suspected Root Cause:
The guide enrichment step in the scheduling pipeline does not use optimistic locking or version checks. The PUT /guides/{id}/schedule endpoint overwrites the entire schedule object instead of merging changes.

Evidence:
- Production logs show 'Guide enrichment data overwritten' warnings
- Elastic logs show concurrent PUT requests to the same guide within 50ms windows

summary: Add Wristband RFID Field to Check-In Record
description:
Add an optional wristbandRfid field to the check-in record schema to support RFID wristband tracking.

The park is rolling out RFID-enabled wristbands for adventure tracking and emergency location services. The check-in service needs to capture the wristband RFID tag ID during the check-in process so that guest location can be tracked throughout the adventure.

Acceptance Criteria:
- POST /check-ins accepts optional wristbandRfid field (string, max 64 chars)
- GET /check-ins/{id} returns wristbandRfid if present
- RFID field is validated against a regex pattern: ^[A-F0-9]{8,16}$
- Duplicate RFID values are rejected (uniqueness constraint per active check-in)
- RFID field is included in the check-in event published to the event bus
- Field is nullable for adventures that don't use wristband tracking

summary: Real-Time Adventure Tracking and Emergency Alerting System
description:
Implement real-time GPS tracking of guests during adventures with automated emergency alerting and rescue dispatch.

Business Context:
NovaTrek currently has no way to track guest positions during active adventures. When emergencies occur (injury, lost guest, severe weather), guides must radio base camp with approximate locations. Search-and-rescue response times average 45 minutes due to imprecise location data. Insurance requirements for 2026 mandate electronic position tracking for all Pattern 2 and Pattern 3 adventures.

Proposed Architecture:
Two new microservices are required:

1. svc-adventure-tracking — Ingests real-time GPS telemetry from guest wristbands and guide devices. Maintains a rolling 4-hour position history per active check-in. Provides geofence boundary monitoring (trail corridor adherence). Exposes position data to authorized consumers via REST and WebSocket.

2. svc-emergency-response — Monitors tracking telemetry for anomalies (geofence breach, SOS button press, prolonged inactivity). Creates and manages emergency incident records. Dispatches alerts to rescue teams and park operations. Coordinates with svc-notifications for guest emergency contact alerts.

Required Events:
- PositionUpdated: Published by svc-adventure-tracking when a new GPS fix is received. Consumed by svc-emergency-response for anomaly detection. High frequency (every 30 seconds per active guest).
- EmergencyAlertRaised: Published by svc-emergency-response when an emergency condition is detected. Consumed by svc-notifications (alert delivery to rescue teams and emergency contacts) and svc-safety-compliance (incident logging and regulatory reporting).

Cross-Service Integration:
- svc-check-in: Provides the active check-in record and wristband RFID linkage (read)
- svc-guest-profiles: Provides emergency contact information for alert delivery (read)
- svc-safety-compliance: Receives incident records for regulatory reporting (write via API)
- svc-notifications: Delivers emergency alerts via SMS and push notification (write via API)
- svc-trail-management: Provides trail geofence boundaries for corridor monitoring (read)
- svc-guide-management: Provides guide device identifiers for position tracking (read)

Acceptance Criteria:
- GPS positions are ingested at 30-second intervals with sub-5-second processing latency
- Geofence breach detection triggers an alert within 60 seconds
- SOS button press triggers an alert within 10 seconds
- Emergency contacts are notified within 2 minutes of alert creation
- Position history is retained for 30 days for incident investigation
- All tracking data is encrypted at rest and in transit
- Tracking is automatically activated at check-in and deactivated at check-out
- System handles up to 2,000 concurrent tracked guests

summary: Guest Loyalty Referral Program with Campaign Management
description:
Build a referral program that allows existing guests to generate shareable referral codes, tracks referral conversions, and issues loyalty rewards — backed by a campaign management service for promotional rules.

Business Context:
NovaTrek's guest acquisition cost has risen 30 percent year-over-year. The loyalty rewards program (svc-loyalty-rewards) currently supports points earning and tier progression but has no referral mechanism. Marketing wants to launch a 'Bring a Friend' initiative where existing guests earn bonus loyalty points when their referrals complete their first adventure. Marketing also needs the ability to run time-limited promotional campaigns (double points weekends, seasonal referral bonuses) without engineering involvement.

Proposed Architecture:
Two new microservices are required:

1. svc-referral-engine — Generates unique referral codes linked to referring guest profiles. Tracks referral code usage: who shared, who redeemed, conversion status. Validates referral eligibility (one redemption per new guest, referring guest must have completed at least one adventure). Calculates referral rewards based on active campaign rules. Publishes events on referral lifecycle transitions.

2. svc-campaign-management — Provides a rule engine for promotional campaigns (referral bonus multipliers, eligibility windows, reward caps). Exposes CRUD API for campaign definitions with scheduling (start date, end date, active flag). Supports A/B test segmentation — different reward tiers for different guest segments. Publishes events when campaigns are activated or expired so dependent services can refresh cached rules.

Required Events:
- ReferralCodeRedeemed: Published by svc-referral-engine when a new guest uses a referral code during their first reservation. Consumed by svc-loyalty-rewards (to credit bonus points to the referring guest) and svc-notifications (to notify the referrer of a successful conversion).
- CampaignActivated: Published by svc-campaign-management when a promotional campaign goes live or expires. Consumed by svc-referral-engine (to refresh reward calculation rules) and svc-trip-catalog (to display promotional badges on eligible adventures).

Cross-Service Integration:
- svc-guest-profiles: Look up referring guest identity and validate guest status (read)
- svc-loyalty-rewards: Credit referral bonus points to referring guest (write via API)
- svc-reservations: Validate that the referred guest actually completed a reservation (read)
- svc-notifications: Send referral success notifications to referring guest (write via API)
- svc-trip-catalog: Display campaign promotional badges on adventure listings (read from svc-campaign-management)
- svc-analytics: Track referral funnel metrics — codes generated, shared, redeemed, converted (write via API)

Acceptance Criteria:
- Guests can generate a unique referral code from their profile page
- Referral codes are 8-character alphanumeric strings, shareable via link or QR code
- Referred guest must be a first-time NovaTrek customer (no existing profile)
- Referral reward is credited only after the referred guest completes their first adventure check-in
- Campaign rules support: bonus multiplier (1x-5x), date range, guest tier eligibility, per-campaign reward cap
- Campaign changes take effect within 5 minutes without service restart
- Referral dashboard shows: codes generated, codes redeemed, pending conversions, total rewards earned
- Anti-fraud: rate limit of 10 referral codes per guest per month, self-referral detection
